verse_specific_anchors: the three-property structure (unmanifest / unthinkable / unchangeable) — each individually mapped to a distinct engineering claim; this is 2.25's specific list of avyakta/acintya/avikārya

the therefore-clause — the engineering analog's 'knowing this — knowing your actual work resides within the contract stratum — perform absolutely no mourning ritual for deprecated source code' is a precise rendering of tasmād evaṁ viditvā nānuśocitum arhasi

the v3→v4 routing scenario operationalises the therefore-clause as a Slack-thread intervention; the four diagnostic questions (same constraints? same SLA? same heuristic? same fallback?) are 2.25-specific operationalisations
verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 2.27 ('for the born, death is certain... you should not grieve' — overlaps the no-grief instruction but lacks the three-property structure), BG 2.30 ('the dweller in the body of all is eternal; therefore you should not grieve' — closest competitor, but uses different properties and addresses 'all beings' rather than the imperishable's three properties), BG 2.11 ('you grieve for those who should not be grieved' — overlaps the no-grief theme but is the chapter's opening statement of the doctrine, not its closing). None has the specific avyakta/acintya/avikārya triple plus the therefore-clause structure. The analog is anchored on 2.25-specific closing-of-the-imperishable-block. PASS.
